message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente Resort Casino Spa Rancho Mirage (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 578 reviews; TripAdvisor 247 reviews (~4.1/5, ranked #10 of 80 in Rancho Mirage); Google review count [UNVERIFIED]
- Sentiment: mostly positive but with notable inconsistency — strong for food/wine/ambiance, mixed on service execution
- Longevity: Established 2001 (~25 years operating); ownership stable — operated by Agua Caliente Casinos, owned by the Agua Caliente Band of Cahuilla Indians
- Credentials: ["Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star (multi-year, seventh consecutive year of Forbes recognition for the property per 2026 announcement)","AAA Four Diamond Award","Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007 (18 consecutive years as of 2025)","OpenTable Diners' Choice Award","Palm Springs Life Best of the Best (self-reported; specific year [UNVERIFIED])","Top 25 Restaurants listing"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly on cuisine-specific queries — 'best steakhouse Palm Springs/Coachella Valley' consistently includes The Steakhouse alongside Mastro's, LG's Prime, The Edge, and Ruth's Chris. Surfaces on 'best fine dining Coachella Valley' especially when 'Forbes Four-Star' is in the query. Does NOT consistently appear in 'date night Rancho Mirage' lists (Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare, The Edge dominate that query) despite Forbes credential. Largely absent from 'fine dining Palm Desert' results because of city boundary.
- Site structured data: Entity page at aguacalientecasinos.com/dining/steakhouse/ returned HTTP 403 to automated fetchers on multiple attempts — strong signal that the site blocks/limits crawler access, which is hostile to agent retrieval. Schema.org JSON-LD presence [UNVERIFIED] due to access block. Restaurant lives as a sub-page of the casino property domain rather than its own restaurant-level structured presence; menu lives at a separate URL (/dining-menu/the-steakhouse-menu-rancho-mirage/). Visit Greater Palm Springs listing exists but renders thin/empty server-side.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here are some of the best date night restaurants in Rancho Mirage for 2026:

## Fine Dining Options

The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage, created by award-winning Chef Darryl Boyd, serves only the finest cuts of American Wagyu, all of which are dry-aged in house and served with a delectable array of starters, sides and signature cocktails. The Edge offers sweeping views of Rancho Mirage and pairs remarkable wagyu steaks with an international wine list.

Wally's Desert Turtle is a Rancho Mirage institution that delivers timeless elegance with white-linen details and pairs refined California cuisine with a glamorous setting—mirrored walls, soft lighting, and crystal accents set the tone for a truly special night out.

Willie's Modern Fare is a chic restaurant serving artfully plated dishes that balance traditional European technique with vibrant California flavors, such as seared scallops with citrus beurre blanc and slow-roasted meats, paired with handcrafted cocktails, and offers warm service with a sleek interior that is ideal for date nights.

## Newer Options

Double Date at Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa is a new dining experience by executive chef Jose Lopez that celebrates California and Italian produce, with everything made in-house including sauces and pastas, elevated with desert-inspired touches.

## Japanese Cuisine

Shabu Shabu Zen offers a cozy Japanese dining experience where you can enjoy fresh, aromatic plum sake and premium cuts of tender Wagyu beef and fresh seafood, earning recognition on Yelp's "100 Best Places to Eat" list in 2017.

content.content:

**TripAdvisor Rating:** 4.1 of 5 bubbles

**Review Count:** 247 reviews

**Ranking:** "#10 of 80 Restaurants in Rancho Mirage"

The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente was established in 2001. The restaurant operates at two locations in Southern California: one at the Agua Caliente Resort Casino Spa in Rancho Mirage and another at Agua Caliente Casino in Palm Springs.

The Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage is the recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award, and has been a winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007. The Palm Springs location has been honored by Wine Spectator consecutively since 2004 for its extensive California wine list.

Based on the search results, here's a comprehensive list of fine dining restaurants in the Coachella Valley for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Restaurants

**Classic French & European:**
- **Copley's On Palm Canyon** - Located in Palm Springs in a historic property that once belonged to Cary Grant, offering a mix of American and European cuisine with seasonal ingredients
- **Chez Pierre Bistro** - Located in Palm Desert, offering authentic French fine dining with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list
- **Le Vallauris** - Offering classic French cuisine in a romantic setting in Palm Springs

**International Cuisine:**

The Coachella Valley offers a wide range of fine dining experiences from sophisticated steakhouses to intimate lounges, catering to diverse culinary preferences.

content.text: Confirmed: The Steakhouse does not surface in "fine dining Palm Desert" results — city boundary confirmed. Sufficient evidence to finalize.

confirms: TripAdvisor 247 reviews, ~4.1/5, ranked #10 of 80 in Rancho Mirage — verified directly on TripAdvisor page
Yelp review count of 578 — confirmed in Yelp result title in search index
Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007 — confirmed by Agua Caliente's own press release; 2025 marks 18 consecutive years for the Rancho Mirage location
Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star recognition for 2026 — confirmed via PR Newswire and Casino Life Magazine
Established 2001 — confirmed in multiple sources; ownership by Agua Caliente Band of Cahuilla Indians via Agua Caliente Casinos confirmed
Site structured-data assessment: aguacalientecasinos.com/dining/steakhouse/ and the separate menu URL both returned HTTP 403 to automated fetchers — confirms hostile-to-agent-retrieval posture
Restaurant lives as a sub-page of the casino property domain (not its own restaurant-level domain) with menu at a separate URL — verified
Surfaces in 'best steakhouse Coachella Valley/Palm Springs' results alongside Mastro's, The Edge, Sullivan's, Eddie V's — confirmed via independent search
Does NOT surface in 'date night Rancho Mirage' lists — confirmed; Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare, The Edge, Double Date dominate that query
Largely absent from 'fine dining Palm Desert' results due to city boundary — confirmed
contradicts: The claim 'seventh consecutive year of Forbes recognition' is for the property (Agua Caliente Casino Rancho Mirage overall), NOT specifically for The Steakhouse's Four-Star rating. The official press release says the property has earned recognition 'for seven consecutive years' and The Steakhouse has held Four-Star 'for multiple consecutive years' without specifying the number. One secondary source (search summary) stated 'Forbes Four-Star rated for six consecutive years' for The Steakhouse, which conflicts with framing the seventh-year stat as belonging to the restaurant. Correct framing: property = 7 consecutive years of recognition; Steakhouse Four-Star = multiple consecutive years (likely 6 for the restaurant specifically).
flagged_as_unverified: Google review count remains unverified (correctly already flagged)

AAA Four Diamond Award — mentioned in promotional copy and a AAA Trip Canvas listing exists, but the current AAA Four Diamond status for this specific year was not independently confirmed via AAA's official diamond awards list
Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' — only self-reported in promotional copy; no independent Palm Springs Life page confirming year was located (correctly already flagged)
OpenTable Diners' Choice — referenced in promotional copy but no independent OpenTable badge year was confirmed
'Top 25 Restaurants' listing — a top25restaurants.com listing exists, but this is a paid/curated directory rather than an editorial ranking; its credibility as a credential is weak
Sentiment characterization (mostly positive but mixed on service) is plausible based on TripAdvisor 4.1/5 and snippets, but not deeply sampled
JSON-LD/schema.org presence on the entity page — unverifiable due to 403 block (correctly already flagged)
Visibility gap score of 6 and 'anchor-reference' convertibility tier are framework-internal judgments not independently testable
input.notes: Core visibility claims (TripAdvisor ranking/reviews, Yelp review count, surfaces in 'best steakhouse Coachella Valley' but not in 'date night Rancho Mirage' or 'fine dining Palm Desert', 403-blocked entity page) all hold up under independent search. Wine Spectator (18 consecutive years since 2007) is solidly documented. The main contradiction is conflation: the 'seventh consecutive year' belongs to the casino property, not specifically to The Steakhouse's Four-Star streak. Several credential items (AAA Four Diamond current-year status, Palm Springs Life Best of the Best, OpenTable Diners' Choice year, Top 25 Restaurants meaningfulness) remain promotional-copy claims without independent issuer-side confirmation. Sources: TripAdvisor, PR Newswire, Casino Life Magazine, Agua Caliente Casinos press release, KNews 94.3.
